name: MaintenanceDeluxe
overview: Premium maintenance overlay with live countdown and rich release notes.
description: Premium cinema-themed maintenance overlay for Jellyfin. Full-screen velours-and-gold overlay with a drifting aurora background, a rotating film-reel logo, crackling ember particles and a brushed-metal card border. Live countdown, time-based progress bar, admin-configurable release notes with quick-add templates and emoji palette. Overlay suppressed on admin pages so admins always have access.

versions.changelog: v0.2.0 - Appearance customization with live preview. New admin section lets you pick accent color, background tint, card opacity, animation speed (off/slow/normal/fast), particle density (none/low/normal/dense), and card border style (full gradient / simple gold / none). A live preview iframe reflects every change instantly without saving. Expand button gives a fullscreen view with toggleable settings panel (H hotkey) and Esc to exit. All colors are now derived from a single accent pick, so the palette stays coherent whatever hue you choose.

v0.1.13 - UI FIX: gold card border was truncated when release notes made the overlay taller than the viewport. The card had its own internal scroll (max-height:92vh) so the ::before gold gradient didn't extend through scrolled content. Fix: overlay scrolls instead of card, card grows to full content height so the border wraps everything, and background layers (blobs, beams, grain, particles) are pinned to viewport with position:fixed.

v0.1.12 - CRITICAL FIX: Save button stopped reacting as soon as any field was modified. Root cause: a TypeError in collectMessages() aborted initDom before the Save handler got wired, because release-note rows shared the .jf-msg-row class with rotation messages and had no .jf-msg-detail. Fix: excluded .jf-rn-row from the rotation collector + defensive null guard in collectRowData.

v0.1.11 - CRITICAL FIX: admin config page was entirely unresponsive since v0.1.8. Root cause: release-note templates had real newlines inside JS string literals, causing the whole inline admin script to fail parsing. Every button, input, and save handler silently died. Now fixed — admin UI fully interactive again, tested with node --check.
